explicit_text: I was born on June 14th, 1963, in Tehran, Iran, and grew up in a middle-class family with educator parents. After the tragic death of my older brother Attila in 1976, my family moved to Frankfurt, Germany, where I finished high school and discovered my talent for soccer. Later, I moved to New York to play college soccer at the State University of New York at Plattsburgh, eventually settling in the United States and starting a family with three children.

explicit_text: I was born and raised in Iran, specifically in Tehran, where my parents worked as educators. In 1976, my family experienced a devastating loss with the death of my older brother, which eventually led us to leave Iran in 1977. My father later returned to Iran during the Iran–Iraq War, but I chose to pursue opportunities abroad to avoid military draft and build a new life.

explicit_text: After leaving Iran in 1977, my family and I settled in Frankfurt, Germany, where I attended high school and learned German despite not speaking it at first. explicit_text: My father, Vahedy Jahanbakhsh, was a school teacher who became a principal and later worked for the Department of Education in Iran. He originally spoke Turkish and was deeply impacted by the loss of my older brother, which led him to decide we should leave Iran. He returned to Iran during the Iran–Iraq War, but advised me to go to Australia to avoid military draft. Sadly, he passed away in 1992 and was against me becoming a U.S. citizen because of Iran's policies on dual citizenship.
